75014.subject: Your AI Second Brain.
75014.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/your-ai-second-brain-genai-works-qlxve?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BmQBX9AuuRNWCbBYMix%2FMZA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2iLGaEnzzdgYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqs4wqzt~sm-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqs4wqzt-sm
  
Welcome back! This edition moves between useful ideas and uncomfortable questions. We start with something practical: how to build your own second brain using Claude and Obsidian. Then we look at ChatGPT’s biggest health-related upgrade so far, and what it could mean for people using AI to understand medical information. After that, we cover a new NIST finding that shows why AI guardrails may never be fully permanent. Finally, we end with a Google DeepMind system that matched physicians across multiple patient visits, raising a bigger question about how AI could support real healthcare over time. In today's Atlas Newsletter: How to build an AI second brain with Claude and Obsidian. Wire the two together into a knowledge system that compounds instead of resetting every session. Improving your health intelligence in ChatGPT. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 Instant reaches frontier-level health performance, free for everyone, with factuality errors down 71%. Your AI guardrails will be bypassed. A NIST proof extends Gödel's theorems to AI safety, and the takeaway reshapes how to secure agents. Towards conversational AI for disease management. DeepMind's AMIE matched primary care physicians over multiple visits, and out-scored them on precision and guideline alignment. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. How to build an AI second brain with Claude and Obsidian that gets smarter every day A new guide making the rounds shows how to turn Claude and Obsidian into a compounding knowledge system, so you stop starting every session from a blank chat window.  It builds on Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki concept.  Obsidian handles local storage and Claude acts as the brain reading and writing to it. How do these pieces fit together? → The stack: Install Obsidian, a free local text store, and Claude Desktop Pro, the engine that reads and writes your files. → The bridge: Add the Local REST API plugin inside Obsidian, copy the key, and connect the two through Claude's MCP framework with a single add command (claude mcp add-json obsidian-vault). → The context: Ask Claude to interview you about your goals, projects and working style, then save its summary to a CLAUDE.md file. That file becomes the memory it loads every time, so it never forgets who you are. → The structure: Create dedicated proj

75012.subject: Crypto miners shrug off selloff
75012.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

==Hi all, happy Wednesday! ==

==South Korea's markets fell 10% and crypto came off with it. BTC held best among the majors at -2.0%, roughly in line with gold, while perps (-6.2%), L2s (-6.1%), and DeFi (-5.9%) anchored the bottom. The only green on the board was equities — crypto miners ripped +4.5%, extending a trade that's now up ~73% on the year even as spot churns sideways. ==

==Below that, we cover Synthetix retiring sUSD — a fixed 4-SNX conversion that hands holders a locked, diluted claim rather than their dollar back.==

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

After the 10% decline in the South Korean markets, risk came off across the board, sorted cleanly by beta. BTC held up best among majors at -2.0%, roughly in line with gold (-2.1%) and inside the NASDAQ's -2.8%, but losses steepened the further out the risk curve you looked: Perps (-6.2%), L2 (-6.1%), and DeFi (-5.9%) anchored the bottom. The only green on the board came from equities. Crypto miners ripped +4.5% while the broader crypto equity cohort held flat-to-positive, a notable divergence from spot tokens.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/010f5f6a-0aa4-4f8f-8b64-81d1efe68bbc/image.png?t=1782307556)
Caption: 

Crypto miners have been the standout trade of 2026, and the YTD chart shows why yesterday's +4.5% pop was just a continuation. The index sits up roughly +73% on the year after a volatile journey to get there: a +30% January run, a full round-trip back to flat through February and March (bottoming near -20% at the end of Q1), then a near-vertical re-rating that began in mid-April and carried the cohort to a +88% peak in late May. Even after fading off those highs, miners remain the highest-beta expression of the AI-and-energy narrative bleeding into crypto and the only sector still printing triple-digit-adjacent gains while spot tokens churn sideways.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/83bea9e3-c350-49d4-8c94-871e505f6eef/image.png?t=1782307556)
Caption: 

Under the hood, that headline index return masks a dispersion of nearly 170 points between the top and bottom components, and the split is almost entirely a function of who has converted idle power capacity into signed hyperscaler contracts. The leaders have effectively stopped being miners and now trade as power-and-compute landlords, valued on

74999.subject: The Anatomy of a Wallet Page
74999.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/the-anatomy-of-a-wallet-page

The last piece in this series covered SnowTrace, Avascan, and SnowScan and when to use each one.
This article focuses on what those tools actually show you. A wallet page is more than a collection of balances and transactions; it’s a record of behavior. Every balance, token, approval, and contract interaction is a clue.
The guiding principle is simple: Always watch for the weird, the unexpected, and the unprecedented.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
Address & Balance Overview
At the top of the page you’ll see the wallet address: a long 0x... string that serves as the account’s identifier on Avalanche’s C-Chain.
Sometimes explorers attach labels to known addresses such as exchanges, protocols, routers, or treasury wallets. If no label exists, all you know is that the address has no publicly recognized identity. Many explorers also allow users to create private name tags for addresses they frequently monitor. These labels are personal notes and do not verify the identity or legitimacy of an address.
Directly beneath the address, you’ll typically find the wallet’s AVAX balance and estimated USD value. By themselves, these figures reveal very little. A wallet may be active, dormant, newly created, or emptied after an attack. The important part is context.
You’ll also see convenience features such as copy buttons and QR codes. These make addresses easier to share but provide no security information.
Normal: A standard address, with or without a label, and balances that align with the wallet’s expected activity.
Warning: Explorer-generated scam warnings, known malicious addresses, or near-zero AVAX balances paired with large numbers of unfamiliar tokens.
Takeaway: The header provides context, not trust. Labels and balances can be useful clues, but the wallet’s activity is what matters most.
Token Holdings
Below the Total balance, you’ll see the wallet’s total balance and the number of assets it holds. Clicking the dropdown arrow expands the asset breakdown, revealing the tokens, NFTs, LP tokens, and other assets associated with the address.
What you find there falls into two categories: assets you acquired intentionally, and assets you didn’t.
Legitimate holdings are usually assets you intentionally acquired, such as stablecoins, LP tokens, governance tokens, or recognized currencies like USDC and WAVAX.
Unfamiliar entries are often spam tokens; worthless assets airdropped to wallets as part of scam campaigns.
The most common red flag here is the presence of unsolicited tokens.
Scammers frequently airdrop worthless tokens to random wallets. The goal is not the token itself but the interaction that follows. Users are encouraged to visit a website, connect a wallet, approve a transaction, or “claim” a reward. Those actions often lead to theft.
